I booked the flight two weeks ago.
The flight is boarding at gate twenty-two.
We are going to take the tram at nine.
One kilo over? All right, I will move it.
The bins are full, so they might check my bag.
One kilo over? I will move it.
Good evening. Here is the menu. Can I get you something to drink while you choose?
Sparkling water for two, please. And could I have a few more minutes? My husband reads the menu very slowly.
Of course. Take your time.
Actually, one question first. What is the most popular main course here?
The sea bass. It is the best thing we make, in my opinion. But it depends on what you like. If you would rather have meat, the beef is very good too.
I am allergic to nuts. Is there anything in the sea bass?
No, nothing at all. But I will check with the kitchen to be sure.
Thank you. Then I would like the sea bass, and my husband would like the beef.
Perfect. And here is your bill, whenever you are ready. Forty-eight euros.
There seems to be a problem with the bill. There is a bottle of wine on it, and we drank water. I think we were overcharged.
You are right, and I am very sorry. That is table nine. I will fix it right now.
Thank you. No problem at all, and the sea bass was worth it.
I booked it two weeks ago, and I would like a window seat, please.
What could I do? All right, then I will do that.
What is the most popular one, in your opinion?
There seems to be a problem. Could you check it again, please?
I used to need help with this. Now it is a piece of cake.

Good morning, ladies and gentlemen. This is the first boarding announcement for the flight to Amsterdam, now boarding at gate twenty-two. Please have your boarding pass and your passport ready in your hand, and not at the bottom of your bag. We are going to board in groups, and I am going to say this only once, so please listen. First: families with small children, and passengers who need a little more time. If that is you, you should come to the desk now. Second: rows twenty to thirty-five, at the back of the aircraft. Third: rows one to nineteen. Please wait for your row. If you come early, you will only stand in the line longer, and the line is already crowded. Now, three things about your bags, and they are important. One: your carry-on goes in the overhead bin, and the small bag goes under the seat in front of you. This aircraft is full today, so there is not much space up there. If the bins are full, we might have to check your carry-on here at the gate. That is free, but you should take your medicine, your documents and your phone out of it first, because you will not see that bag again until Amsterdam. Two: a lot of passengers today are travelling with two carry-on bags, and the limit is one. Please do not make us stop the line. Three: if your bag is damaged when it arrives, do not go home. You should go to the desk in the baggage hall in Amsterdam before you leave the airport, because after you leave, we cannot help you. Our flight time today is two hours and fifty minutes. The weather in Amsterdam is nine degrees and grey, so I would rather be here. Thank you very much, and welcome aboard.

Excuse me. Is this the line for Amsterdam? Thank you. My name is Yuko. May I stand here with you? Twenty minutes is a long time at my age. I am seventy-one. I know, I know. Everybody says I do not look seventy-one, and I always give them the same answer: it depends on the day! Can I ask you something? Your English is good. Where did you learn it? ... Ten lessons. Ten lessons! Then listen to me, because your story is almost my story. I am from Osaka, and I started English when I was sixty-six. Sixty-six! At school I learned nothing. I used to read a little, once in a while, but I never spoke. Not one sentence, for fifty years. And then I retired, and my children said: Mother, we are busy, we have our own lives, go with your sister. And I was angry. I was angry for one whole week! And then I was not angry any more, because they were right. My first trip alone was three days in Seoul, and I cried in the hotel on the first night. In my opinion that is completely normal, and nobody tells you that part. But on the third day I ordered my own dinner. I asked the waiter what he would recommend, he told me, and I understood him. Such a small thing. It is a piece of cake now. That night it was the biggest thing in the world. Now I go somewhere twice a year. And I still make mistakes, of course. Yesterday I said something wrong to a taxi driver here in Lisbon, and he laughed. And I laughed too! I would rather laugh with him than say nothing at all. That is the whole secret, and it took me sixty-six years to learn it. People ask me, Yuko, at seventy-one, why? It is too late. I disagree. It is worth it, every single time. I am looking forward to Amsterdam. I have never been. Look, they are calling our rows. Come on.

I am looking forward to seeing Amsterdam.
You were lucky. I disagree. I was not lucky. I was ready.
I booked it two weeks ago, and I would like a window seat, please.
What could I do? All right, then I will do that.
In my opinion that is a lot of money, but it is worth it.
